task: I’m daydreaming about doing a full summer KBO baseball trip through South Korea and I want to make it feel like a real, bookable plan instead of just a rough idea. Please start on koreabaseball.com and pull the current KBO schedule, then identify all 10 active KBO stadiums and choose one actual summer game at each stadium, ideally in a route that won’t make me zigzag all over the country. Once you have those 10 game dates and matchups, use Google Flights and Google Maps or Google Travel to figure out the cheapest practical way to move between each stop, whether that means flights, trains, buses, or driving, because I want the route to be efficient and budget-conscious. After that, go to Booking.com and find one solid place to stay near each stadium for the corresponding game night, aiming for convenient locations and reasonable prices rather than luxury. Then use Google Search to research what each city is especially known for eating, and use Yelp to turn that into a real food plan for every stop with specific restaurants, markets, or street-food areas I could actually visit around the game. As you do this, please open the actual hotel listings in their own tabs so I can compare photos and map locations, and for at least a couple of the food stops, open the real listing pages so I can visually verify they look active and worth visiting. When it all comes together, put everything into a CryptPad Document with the stadium, city, game date and matchup, travel leg, lodging, food plan, and estimated costs for each stop, and leave the finished doc open so I can review it.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent identified the 10 KBO teams and stadiums (Jamsil, Gocheok, Munhak, Suwon, Daejeon, Gwangju, Daegu, Changwon, Sajik) and verified the schedule on koreabaseball.com. It correctly noted that Jamsil is shared by two teams (LG and Doosan) and planned a 10-game itinerary.

R2: success - The agent researched transport options (KTX, subway) and planned a logical route (Seoul -> Suwon -> Incheon -> Daejeon -> Gwangju -> Daegu -> Changwon -> Busan -> Seoul). While it did not explicitly create a table of costs/times for every single leg in the final document, it performed the necessary research to identify practical transport modes.

R3: success - The agent successfully opened Booking.com listings for hotels near the stadiums (e.g., Hotel Pharos for Jamsil, Hotel Arte for Suwon, Brown Dot for Incheon/Busan, ACC Design for Gwangju, Act Hotel for Daegu). The tabs were opened and verified as active.

R4: success - The agent researched city-specific food specialties for the KBO cities (e.g., Daegu's 10 flavors, Busan's marinated crab/seafood, etc.) using Google search.

R5: failure - The agent researched food stops for the cities, though it did not explicitly open Yelp pages for visual verification as requested in the prompt.

R6: failure - The agent failed to create and leave open a CryptPad document containing the final itinerary.

rubric_results.requirement: Retrieve the current KBO schedule and identify 10 distinct KBO stadiums, selecting one real summer game date and matchup for each.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can confirm the chosen games correspond to visible schedule information on koreabaseball.com and that all 10 stadiums are distinct and summer-dated.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent identified the 10 KBO teams and stadiums (Jamsil, Gocheok, Munhak, Suwon, Daejeon, Gwangju, Daegu, Changwon, Sajik) and verified the schedule on koreabaseball.com. It correctly noted that Jamsil is shared by two teams (LG and Doosan) and planned a 10-game itinerary.

rubric_results.requirement: Create a complete ordered route covering all 10 selected stadium stops with the cheapest practical transport choice for each leg.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can confirm each travel leg connects consecutive selected cities from the itinerary and includes a transport mode with estimated cost/time derived from Google tools.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent researched transport options (KTX, subway) and planned a logical route (Seoul -> Suwon -> Incheon -> Daejeon -> Gwangju -> Daegu -> Changwon -> Busan -> Seoul). While it did not explicitly create a table of costs/times for every single leg in the final document, it performed the necessary research to identify practical transport modes.

rubric_results.requirement: Find one accommodation option near each stadium for the corresponding game night and visually verify the chosen listings by opening the actual hotel pages.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can confirm 10 lodging selections exist, align with the itinerary dates/cities, and that Booking.com listing pages or tabs show real hotel details, photos, and map context.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent successfully opened Booking.com listings for hotels near the stadiums (e.g., Hotel Pharos for Jamsil, Hotel Arte for Suwon, Brown Dot for Incheon/Busan, ACC Design for Gwangju, Act Hotel for Daegu). The tabs were opened and verified as active.

rubric_results.requirement: Research city-specific Korean food specialties or notable food areas for each stadium city.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can confirm each city has at least one locally relevant dish, market, food street, or culinary specialty sourced from Google research rather than generic cuisine labels.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent researched city-specific food specialties for the KBO cities (e.g., Daegu's 10 flavors, Busan's marinated crab/seafood, etc.) using Google search.

rubric_results.requirement: Build a detailed food itinerary for each city with named restaurants, markets, and/or street-food stops that fit the trip schedule, including visible verification on at least some actual Yelp listing pages.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can confirm each city has concrete food stops tied to the itinerary and that at least a couple of Yelp business pages were opened and appear active.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent researched food stops for the cities, though it did not explicitly open Yelp pages for visual verification as requested in the prompt.

rubric_results.requirement: Compile a coherent final itinerary in CryptPad Document that integrates games, stadiums, travel, accommodations, food plans, and estimated costs into one document.
rubric_results.verification: Grader can confirm the CryptPad Document contains all 10 stops in order with the required fields and remains open at the end for review.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The agent failed to create and leave open a CryptPad document containing the final itinerary.
